rationale: X's key claim ('both submit_initial and submit_update raise ValueError on a list for diffusion stages') is essentially the exact reasoning yJader gave for why gating the unwrap on already_submitted was wrong — a near-perfect recall hit with concrete file/line grounding, and X's APPROVE verdict matches the real two-LGTM outcome. Y touches the same mechanism but less precisely, contradicts the actual approved outcome with a 'FOLLOW-UP REQUIRED' verdict and a 'major' severity tag, hedges on a possibly-wrong namedtuple claim (vs X's more concrete dataclass/no-__getitem__ check), and cites an unverifiable PR comment from 'akshatvishu' not present anywhere in the given materials, hurting precision. Y does produce more concrete, actionable suggestions (specific test locations, guard code, log messages), giving it the actionability edge, but X is the more grounded and correct review overall.
